message:
fix(fnp-python): rfft native-eligibility ndim fallback misclassified nested lists (yffc self-review)

Sibling defect to commit 1c45127 (searchsorted scalar misclassification).
Self-review on commit 37c7870 (yffc) found that the new rfft native-
eligibility check used `unwrap_or(1)` when `a.ndim` was unavailable.
That made nested-list inputs like `[[1,2,3,4],[5,6,7,8]]` (Python
lists have no `ndim` attribute) take the 1-D-only native path, where
UFuncArray::rfft uses `shape[0]` as the FFT length and silently
produces wrong output where numpy would compute rfft along the last
axis of a 2-D input.

Fix: when `a.getattr("ndim")` fails, route through `numpy.asarray`
to derive the actual rank. Sequence inputs (Python lists/tuples)
correctly resolve their dimensionality and route to the right path;
truly opaque objects fall through to the numpy passthrough via
`unwrap_or(-1)` (which guarantees `native_eligible == false`).

Also adds a MUST regression case to conformance_fft.rs:
  - fft-rfft-nested-list-2d-input

Final fft conformance after the patch:
  | fft | MUST 19/19 (100.0%) | SHOULD 5/5 (100.0%) | MAY 2/2 (100.0%) |

br-franken_numpy-yffc

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -461,6 +461,31 @@ fn conformance_fft_matrix() {
                 Ok(Some(kw))
             },
         );
+        // Regression: prior native-eligibility ndim fallback was
+        // `unwrap_or(1)`, which let nested-list 2-D inputs (no `ndim`
+        // attribute) take the 1-D-only native path and silently produce
+        // wrong output. MUST tier so the fix can't silently regress.
+        run_case(
+            py,
+            &fft,
+            &np_fft,
+            "fft-rfft-nested-list-2d-input",
+            "rfft",
+            RequirementLevel::Must,
+            CompareMode::Close,
+            t,
+            |py| {
+                let nested = pyo3::types::PyList::new(
+                    py,
+                    [
+                        pyo3::types::PyList::new(py, [1.0_f64, 2.0, 3.0, 4.0])?,
+                        pyo3::types::PyList::new(py, [5.0_f64, 6.0, 7.0, 8.0])?,
+                    ],
+                )?;
+                PyTuple::new(py, [nested.into_any()])
+            },
+            no_kwargs,
+        );
         run_case(
             py,
             &fft,
